finding.location: normalize() — full function body (lines 1-58); no sanitization of CR/LF in any replace/join step
finding.reachability: Any caller that passes user-controlled strings to urlJoin() and then uses the returned value directly in an HTTP response header (e.g., res.setHeader('Location', urlJoin(...))) is reachable; very common server-side redirect pattern.
finding.justification: The function performs no stripping or encoding of carriage-return (\r) or line-feed (\n) characters at any point; a string like urlJoin('https://example.com', 'path\r\nX-Evil: injected') passes through intact, enabling HTTP response splitting / header injection if the result is placed in a Location or Set-Cookie header.

detection_pattern.section_name: Header Injection: CRLF passthrough in URL builder
detection_pattern.description: Detects patterns where a URL-joining call result flows into a header-setting call without CRLF sanitization; dangerous when user input is a component.

finding.location: normalize() lines 16-19 — protocol normalization replaces any protocol's slashes but never validates the scheme; urlJoin() line 63 accepts any first element
finding.reachability: Any caller that accepts user-controlled input as the first argument (protocol/base) or mixes a fixed dangerous protocol with user-controlled path; reachable in template/link-generation code.
finding.justification: The regex /^([^/:]+):\/*/ on lines 17/19 normalizes ANY protocol without a denylist, so urlJoin('javascript:', 'alert(1)') produces 'javascript://alert(1)' and urlJoin('data:', 'text/html,') produces 'data://text/html,...'; if the returned string is assigned to an href, src, or action attribute, this directly enables XSS.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: dangerous protocol passthrough in URL join
detection_pattern.description: Flags calls to url-join (or similar concatenators) that do not validate the resulting protocol before the URL is used in a DOM or HTML context; the library normalizes all schemes equally, including javascript: and data:.

finding.location: normalize() lines 22-44 — the loop strips leading/trailing slashes from components but does not canonicalize '..' segments
finding.reachability: Callers that construct file-system paths or internal API paths from urlJoin output and pass the result to fs.* or path.resolve without further normalization; less common but possible in Node.js file-serving code.
finding.justification: urlJoin('https://example.com/api', '../../../etc/passwd') produces 'https://example.com/api/../../../etc/passwd' with no removal of dot-dot segments; downstream consumers that treat the path portion as a local filesystem path without normalization are vulnerable to path traversal.

detection_pattern.section_name: Path Traversal: dot-dot passthrough in URL builder
detection_pattern.description: Catches URL-join results used in file-path or internal routing contexts without dot-dot normalization; the library does not resolve '..' segments.
